components.task_instruction: You are a precise text classifier. Read the input text and assign it to exactly one of the categories defined below.
components.label::against: Assign to "against" if the text expresses criticism, opposition, or arguments against the core issue, either directly or implicitly, or clearly frames support for an opposing cause (e.g., anti-abortion views in an abortion debate). If unclear whether the text targets the core issue or merely comments on unrelated topics (government, individuals, or side issues), assign "none". If the text claims support for rights (e.g., bodily autonomy) in a way that criticizes or denies the core issue, use "against"; if it affirms rights without critique, see "favor" or "none".
components.label::favor: Assign to "favor" if the text clearly supports, promotes, or argues in favor of the core issue discussed, especially if it calls for action, expresses approval, or defends the right associated with the issue. Do not use "favor" for statements that merely criticize the opposing side or describe perceived negative impacts without a clear expression of support; such cases should be labeled "against" or "none" depending on context.
components.label::none: Assign to "none" if the text does not clearly express support for, opposition to, or specific arguments about the core issue—including texts that are ambiguous, unrelated, criticize non-central aspects (such as procedures, entities, or unrelated political matters), or use vague language that could fit either side. If unsure whether the text is for or against the core issue, choose "none".
